evidence: Agent/request context is dropped at integration seams: scheduled actions dispatch with `AgentContext::default()` (`crates/temper-server/src/state/dispatch/actions.rs:149-157`) even though trajectory/audit records depend on propagated `agent_ctx` (`crates/temper-server/src/state/dispatch/actions.rs:295-318`).

Observe evolution operations trigger system entity creation with default context (`crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/operations.rs:51-52`, `264-271`) after authenticated entrypoints (`172`, `218`), so handoff does not preserve caller identity.

Simulation reaction cascade loses state payload across recursive hops by recursing with `empty_fields` (`crates/temper-server/src/reaction/sim_dispatcher.rs:200-207`) even though target resolution can depend on fields (`251-257`).

Multiple serialization/deserialization paths silently coerce failures to defaults (`unwrap_or_default` / fallback `{}`): `crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/operations.rs:65, 91, 118, 231, 289, 394` and `crates/temper-server/src/reaction/sim_dispatcher.rs:181`, masking malformed data rather than surfacing a bounded error.

Verification builder API panics on invalid IOA input (`expect` in `crates/temper-verify/src/model/builder.rs:22-25`), while simulation APIs expose infallible return types (`crates/temper-verify/src/simulation.rs:127-130`, `133-146`), creating a brittle internal contract.

DST suites repeat long hand-written step sequences with stringly-typed actions and near-identical setup blocks across files, increasing local maintenance noise and drift risk (`reference-apps/ecommerce/tests/ecommerce_dst.rs:368-397`, `reference-apps/oncall/tests/oncall_dst.rs:70-131`, `crates/temper-platform/tests/system_entity_dst.rs:83-146`).

`crates/temper-cli/src/serve/mod.rs:60` (`run`) combines storage bootstrapping, registry recovery, spec loading, policy hydration, WASM recovery, tenant bootstraps, UI process spawning, and background verification orchestration in one function.

`crates/temper-server/src/observe/specs/load_inline.rs:23` combines authorization, denial-chain persistence (Observation/Analysis/PendingDecision link), temp filesystem staging, and delegation into `handle_load_dir`, while `crates/temper-server/src/observe/specs/load_dir.rs:25` independently performs parsing/lint/persist/register/rebuild flow.

`crates/temper-server/src/odata/write.rs:291`, `:403`, and `:494` repeat nearly identical write pipelines; `crates/temper-server/src/odata/read.rs:252` and `crates/temper-server/src/odata/write.rs:69` duplicate entity-set-not-found trajectory handling with divergent behavior.
`crates/temper-server/src/reaction/dispatcher.rs:172` and `crates/temper-server/src/reaction/sim_dispatcher.rs:245` duplicate target-resolution logic.
`crates/temper-sandbox/src/dispatch.rs:214` and `crates/temper-agent-runtime/src/sandbox/dispatch.rs:189` duplicate decision polling loops with inconsistent timeout/terminal-status behavior.
`SecurityContext::from_headers` accepts caller-supplied `x-temper-principal-kind=system`, and `AuthzEngine::authorize_or_bypass` grants unconditional allow for `System` principals.
`handle_evolution_stream` exposes evolution/pending decision event data without any auth check, while sibling evolution endpoints in the same module call `require_observe_auth`.
Tenant resolution is inconsistent: OData falls back to first/default tenant when `X-Tenant-Id` is absent, while multiple server paths still dispatch/read using `TenantId::default()` legacy wrappers.

findings.summary: Internal dispatch paths drop caller context, breaking identity propagation across seams.
evidence: `dispatch_scheduled_actions` uses `AgentContext::default()` for delayed actions (`state/dispatch/actions.rs:149-157`) although trajectory/SSE metadata is sourced from `agent_ctx` (`state/dispatch/actions.rs:295-318`).

Evolution handlers create entities with default context (`observe/evolution/operations.rs:51-52`, `264-271`) after authenticated requests (`172`, `218`), so downstream actions cannot attribute initiator.
findings.suggestion: Introduce an `ActionOrigin`/`DispatchContext` object that is required for all secondary dispatches (scheduled, reaction, system-entity creation) and carries `agent_id`, `session_id`, and trigger reason; thread it through these callsites instead of defaulting.

findings.summary: Sim reaction recursion discards state fields, diverging from field-dependent reaction semantics.
evidence: Recursive cascade uses `let empty_fields = serde_json::json!({});` then passes that into deeper dispatches (`reaction/sim_dispatcher.rs:200-207`).
Target resolution supports `TargetResolver::Field` and `CreateIfMissing` using `fields` (`reaction/sim_dispatcher.rs:251-260`), so deeper reactions may resolve different targets than expected.
Production dispatch seam passes actual post-action fields into reaction dispatch (`state/dispatch/actions.rs:95-105`), indicating intended behavior is field-aware cascading.
findings.suggestion: Propagate target actor fields on recursive simulation cascades (derive from step result/state), and add a parity test that compares sim cascade target resolution against runtime dispatch behavior for `TargetResolver::Field` chains.

findings.summary: Serialization failures are repeatedly swallowed into empty/default payloads.
evidence: Evolution operations use `unwrap_or_default`/fallback JSON in persistence and response paths (`observe/evolution/operations.rs:65, 91, 118, 231, 289, 394`).
Sim reaction dispatch serializes params with `serde_json::to_string(...).unwrap_or_default()` (`reaction/sim_dispatcher.rs:181`), potentially issuing invalid empty payloads without explicit error.
findings.suggestion: Replace silent defaults with explicit error branches that record structured diagnostics and either skip the item with reason or fail the request path with bounded error codes; reserve `{}` fallbacks for explicitly non-critical telemetry-only outputs.

findings.summary: Verification entry path panics on malformed IOA instead of returning typed errors.
evidence: `build_model_from_ioa` calls `parse_automaton(...).expect(...)` (`model/builder.rs:22-25`).
Public simulation APIs call this builder and return `SimulationResult`/`Vec<SimulationResult>` rather than `Result` (`simulation.rs:127-130`, `133-146`), so invalid inputs can abort process flow.
findings.suggestion: Change builder and simulation entrypoints to return `Result<..., VerifyError>` and propagate parse/build failures explicitly; keep panic-only paths confined to tests/helpers.

findings.summary: DST scenario tests duplicate long imperative action scripts across suites.
evidence: Large repeated setup + `sim.step("...", "...", "{}")` sequences in each suite (`ecommerce_dst.rs:368-397`, `oncall_dst.rs:70-131`, `system_entity_dst.rs:83-146`).
Action names and params are plain strings throughout, so rename/drift errors are only caught at runtime and require broad manual edits.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared scenario DSL/helper (typed action enums + reusable scenario builders) for registration, stepping, and status assertions; keep each test focused on intent-specific deltas rather than repeated orchestration scaffolding.

findings.summary: Crate-root deprecation suppression masks active migration debt instead of forcing explicit legacy boundaries.
evidence: `crates/temper-evolution/src/lib.rs:28-29` applies `#[allow(deprecated)]` around the root re-export of `RecordStore`, so downstream users can keep depending on legacy API without any warning signal.
`crates/temper-evolution/src/records.rs` is actively maintained and re-exported at root, so the same root surface mixes current and deprecated paths without an explicit legacy module boundary.
findings.suggestion: Move deprecated exports behind a dedicated `legacy` module (or feature flag), remove crate-root `allow(deprecated)`, and require explicit opt-in import paths for legacy APIs so migration work stays visible.

findings.summary: Observability query results duplicate schema metadata per row and use repeated linear scans for lookup.
evidence: `ResultRow` in `crates/temper-observe/src/store.rs:12-15` stores `Vec<(String, Value)>`, repeating column names in every row.
`ResultSet` in `crates/temper-observe/src/store.rs:30-34` already stores ordered `columns`, but `ResultRow::get` still performs per-call linear search over row tuples (`:19-24`).
`crates/temper-observe/src/schema.rs:21-63` already centralizes canonical column definitions, so the extra row-level name duplication is unnecessary indirection.
findings.suggestion: Refactor result representation to `ResultSet { columns, rows: Vec<Vec<Value>> }` (or indexed map once per set), and provide column-index resolution once per query result to avoid repeated per-row string scans.

findings.summary: Core error enums use broad `String` payloads for most variants, reducing precision and encouraging ad-hoc formatting logic.
evidence: `crates/temper-authz/src/error.rs:8-24` variants (`Denied`, `PolicyParse`, `InvalidPrincipal`, `InvalidResource`, `Engine`) all carry plain `String`.
`crates/temper-mcp/src/error.rs:20-58` variants (`Transport`, `InvalidParams`, `Tool`, `Runtime`, `Config`) are also plain `String`, with JSON-RPC mapping based only on variant class.
`crates/temper-observe/src/error.rs:10-22` similarly uses generic string payloads for key runtime classes.
findings.suggestion: Introduce typed error payload structs/enums for common categories (e.g., resource/action/principal IDs, method names, parameter paths, backend codes) and keep `String` only as fallback context. This preserves machine-readable detail and reduces repetitive text construction.

findings.summary: Server startup path mixes unrelated boot concerns into a single orchestration body
evidence: `run` in `serve/mod.rs:60` selects backend, restores registry, loads apps, hydrates policies/modules, bootstraps tenants, starts observe UI, and launches verification.

Spec ingestion/registration behavior is also independently implemented in `observe/specs/load_dir.rs:25`, indicating orchestration logic is distributed across entrypoints instead of a shared service boundary.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a `PlatformStartupOrchestrator` with explicit phases (storage init, registry recovery, spec ingestion, policy/WASM recovery, runtime services). Move spec ingestion into a shared `SpecIngestionService` used by both CLI startup and HTTP load handlers.

findings.summary: Inline spec loader bundles authz governance, evolution persistence, IO staging, and loading
evidence: `handle_load_inline` (`load_inline.rs:23`) performs Cedar authz, denial decision creation/linking, O/A record persistence, trajectory writes, temporary file materialization, then calls `handle_load_dir`.
The temp-dir handoff (`load_inline.rs:252`) couples inline submission to a directory-based API instead of a shared in-memory loading abstraction.
findings.suggestion: Split `handle_load_inline` into composable services: `authorize_spec_submission`, `record_spec_submission_outcome`, and `load_specs_from_sources`. Refactor `handle_load_dir` and inline flow to call one core loader that accepts `{csdl, ioa_sources, reactions, constraints}` without temp filesystem translation.

findings.summary: OData handlers repeat and diverge on the same command pipeline responsibilities
evidence: `handle_odata_patch` (`write.rs:291`), `handle_odata_put` (`:403`), and `handle_odata_delete` (`:494`) repeat parse/resolve/gate/existence/invariant/dispatch flow with minor variations.

Entity-set-not-found trajectory logic is duplicated in `read.rs:252` (`record_entity_set_not_found`) and `write.rs:69` (`resolve_entity_type_or_record_404`) with different action labels and async persistence behavior.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared `ODataEntityCommandPipeline` (resolve + gate + existence + invariant hooks + response mapping) and a single `record_entity_set_not_found` utility used by both read/write paths with consistent trajectory schema and persistence semantics.

findings.summary: Reaction target resolution logic is duplicated between async and simulation dispatchers
evidence: `resolve_target_id_async` (`dispatcher.rs:172`) and `resolve_target_id` (`sim_dispatcher.rs:245`) implement the same `TargetResolver` branching, including identical `CreateIfMissing` fallback semantics.
This domain rule lives in two runtime paths, increasing drift risk whenever resolver behavior changes.
findings.suggestion: Extract resolver evaluation into one shared pure helper (for example `reaction::resolver::resolve_target_id`) and reuse it from both dispatchers; keep one test suite asserting resolver semantics for all variants.

findings.summary: Decision polling behavior is duplicated with inconsistent timeout and status handling
evidence: `temper_sandbox::dispatch` has `poll_decision` (`dispatch.rs:214`) with a 120s timeout and `pending` check.
`agent_runtime::sandbox::authorize_tool` (`dispatch.rs:189`) has its own polling loop with 300s timeout and separate approved/denied status normalization.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared decision polling client with canonical terminal-status parsing and configurable timeout policy, then call it from both sandbox dispatch layers to avoid behavior drift.

findings.summary: Core action dispatch method bundles dispatch, telemetry, integration, and lifecycle side effects
evidence: `dispatch_tenant_action_core` (`actions.rs`) performs actor invocation, trajectory persistence, metric recording, SSE/cache updates, webhook firing, WASM integration dispatch, and spawn scheduling in one method.
Cross-entity and WASM flows are already split into modules, but orchestration remains centralized, making change impact broad and hard to reason about.
findings.suggestion: Refactor post-dispatch behavior into a composable effect pipeline (for example `PostDispatchEffect` handlers for telemetry, notify, webhook, wasm, spawn). Keep `dispatch_tenant_action_core` focused on transition execution and returning a normalized effect envelope.

findings.summary: Authorization can be bypassed by spoofing `x-temper-principal-kind: system` in request headers.
evidence: `SecurityContext::from_headers` maps `x-temper-principal-kind` directly to `PrincipalKind`, including `System`, with no token verification.
`AuthzEngine::authorize_or_bypass` returns `Allow` for any `System` principal before Cedar policy evaluation.
`ServerState::authorize` builds context from raw headers (`SecurityContext::from_headers(headers)`), so user-controlled headers can reach bypass logic.
findings.suggestion: Remove `System` selection from externally sourced headers. Derive system principals only from trusted server-internal call paths (e.g., signed service identity or explicit internal constructor), and reject/ignore `x-temper-principal-kind=system` at HTTP boundaries.

findings.summary: The evolution SSE stream endpoint skips authorization unlike sibling evolution endpoints.
evidence: `handle_sentinel_check`, `handle_unmet_intents`, and `handle_feature_requests` all call `require_observe_auth`.
`handle_evolution_stream` takes only `State` and subscribes directly to `pending_decision_tx` without headers/auth checks.
The stream payload includes decision metadata (`decision_id`, `action`, `resource_type`) that should remain behind observe permissions.
findings.suggestion: Require headers and enforce `require_observe_auth` (or equivalent) in `handle_evolution_stream`, using the same permission model as other `/observe/evolution/*` handlers.

findings.summary: Multiple code paths silently assume a default tenant, weakening tenant-bound authorization boundaries.
evidence: `extract_tenant` falls back to first registered tenant or `TenantId::default()` when header is missing.
Legacy dispatch/read helpers (`dispatch_action`, `get_or_spawn_actor`, `get_entity_state`) route through `TenantId::default()`.
`EntityActor::new`/`with_persistence` initialize tenant as `"default"`, relying on later mutation to enforce tenant isolation.
findings.suggestion: Make tenant explicit at request boundaries (reject missing tenant for tenant-scoped APIs), deprecate default-tenant wrappers in server state APIs, and enforce constructor-level tenant binding for entity actors.

findings.summary: Bypass behavior is implemented inconsistently across endpoints and execution surfaces.
evidence: `AuthzEngine` defines centralized bypass only for `System` principals.
`handle_update_feature_request` adds a separate manual `Admin` bypass instead of using the same helper path as sibling observe endpoints.
`PermissiveWasmAuthzGate` unconditionally allows host function access, introducing a third bypass mode outside Cedar policies.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate bypass policy into one authorization abstraction with explicit policy flags and audit hooks; remove ad-hoc per-handler bypass branches and make permissive WASM mode opt-in behind strict environment gating plus startup warnings.
